    def test_non_empty_value_written_bare(self, tmp_path):

actions = merge_dotenv(tmp_path, values)

    def test_handles_commented_existing_key(self, tmp_path):

"""A commented-out key (# OSOJI_TOKEN=) should NOT count as 'existing'."""
        (tmp_path / ".env").write_text("# OSOJI_TOKEN=\n")
        values = {"OSOJI_TOKEN": "tok123"}
        actions = merge_dotenv(tmp_path, values)
        content = (tmp_path / ".env").read_text()

actions = merge_dotenv(tmp_path, values)
        content = (tmp_path / ".env").read_text()
        assert "OSOJI_TOKEN=tok123" in content
        added = [a for a in actions if a["action"] == "added"]
        assert len(added) == 1

"""Writing an empty value when a commented-out placeholder exists should skip."""
        (tmp_path / ".env").write_text("# OSOJI_TOKEN=\n")
        values = {"OSOJI_TOKEN": ""}
        actions = merge_dotenv(tmp_path, values)
        content = (tmp_path / ".env").read_text()

content = (tmp_path / ".env").read_text()
        # Should NOT add a second placeholder
        assert content.count("OSOJI_TOKEN") == 1
        skipped = [a for a in actions if a["action"] == "skipped"]
        assert len(skipped) == 1

`claude -p` cannot round-trip custom tool calls, so the in-process
    Triage exploration loop has no transport on the claude-code provider.
    The Agent SDK wraps the same CLI (Max OAuth auth carries over) and
    dispatches MCP tool calls to in-process callbacks — ExplorationExecutor

# tests\test_init.py

## tests/test_init.py

Test suite for `osoji/init.py` — the module that handles `osoji init` project scaffolding. Covers TOML serialization, file merge operations, the top-level `run_init` orchestrator, and CLI integration via Click's test runner.

### Test Classes

**`TestSerializeToml` (L17–55)**
Tests `_serialize_toml(dict) → str`. Verifies:
- Empty dict → empty string (L18–19)
- Round-trip fidelity via `tomllib.loads()` (L22–30, L41–49)
- Scalar keys appear before table sections in output (L31–32)
- Nested sections render as `[parent.child]` headers (L34–39)
- String escaping for quotes and backslashes (L51–55)

**`TestMergeGitignore` (L58–87)**
Tests `merge_gitignore(root: Path) → list[dict]`. Verifies:
- Creates `.gitignore` with `.osoji/`, `.osoji.local.toml`, `.env` when missing (L59–66)
- Returns action dicts with `{"action": "added"|"skipped"}` entries (L66, L73–76)
- Skips lines already present; adds only missing lines (L68–76)
- Appends new entries under a `# Osoji\n` section separator when file has existing content (L83–87)

**`TestMergeDotenv` (L90–144)**
Tests `merge_dotenv(root: Path, values: dict) → list[dict]`. Verifies:
- Empty-value entries written as `# KEY=` comment placeholders; non-empty as bare `KEY=val` (L91–98, L112–123)
- Existing keys (uncommented) are skipped; action dict includes `"key"` field (L100–110)
- A commented placeholder `# KEY=` does NOT count as existing for non-empty writes — will be added again (L125–133)
- A commented placeholder `# KEY=` DOES skip if writing an empty value (avoids duplicate comments) (L135–144)

**`TestMergeProjectToml` (L147–187)**
Tests `merge_project_toml(root: Path, project_slug: str|None) → list[dict]`. Verifies:
- Creates `.osoji.toml` with `[push]\nproject = "..."` when missing (L148–154)
- Skips when `push.project` already set; preserves existing value (L156–162)
- Merges `project` k

finding.triage_reasoning: Reality: Three internal project string literals are shared between `init.py` and its tests as bare strings with no shared constant. (1) `"OSOJI_TOKEN"` is the project's own env-var name, produced in `init.py`'s `merge_dotenv` call and asserted in 10+ test locations — a named project obligation. (2) `"claude-code"` is the project's own internal provider registry key, used in conditional branches in `init.py` and asserted in tests; the manifest adjudication notes confirm it is an internal key ('not ecosystem'). (3) `"default_provider"` is the TOML key name written by `init.py` and checked in tests. All three are clearly-related producer-consumer pairs with no shared constant. Significance: a rename in `init.py` silently breaks tests. Actionability: extract `PROVIDER_CLAUDE_CODE = "claude-code"`, `ENV_KEY_OSOJI_TOKEN = "OSOJI_TOKEN"`, and `TOML_KEY_DEFAULT_PROVIDER = "default_provider"` into a shared constants module.
finding.suggested_fix: Extract `PROVIDER_CLAUDE_CODE = "claude-code"`, `ENV_KEY_OSOJI_TOKEN = "OSOJI_TOKEN"`, and `TOML_KEY_DEFAULT_PROVIDER = "default_provider"` into a shared constants module (e.g., `src/osoji/constants.py`) and import them in both `init.py` and `tests/test_init.py`.
